raw_input:
[4.0/5] Cute and soft. This set of brushes is perfect for travel or a great beginner set for someone just starting to get into makeup. I was surprised at how soft the synthetic brushes on my skin. I am picky about what I use on my face and these brushes past my softness test. 7 different pieces, the only one I rarely use is the sponge tip, but that is just preference. Cute set! Always remember to wash your brushes before use.
Sample provided [5.0/5] Perfect gift Love this set
The color purple is outstanding and great
This product would make a great gift for your friends and family
A great set to start purchasing for the upcoming holiday [5.0/5] Nice I really like this. It was affordable and I use the brushes daily. Was a good purchase! [2.0/5] Two Stars Very cheap [4.0/5] good for teens I purchased these as a gift for my daughter. The color is cute and so is the case they come in. The brushes do the job they are meant to do, but are a little rough and don't evenly spread the makeup. [5.0/5] Great deal The brushes are really cute and well made. They are soft and I've experienced no shedding. I've washed them and they look brand new. Great deal for the price. [5.0/5] Very soft Product arrived as promised package securely without damage. The carry case is a plastic leather vinyl type bag. The brushes inside are much softer than I anticipated that they would be. They hold makeup powder very fine and provide a light dusting of your product without over usage. I originally purchased this with the intention of providing it to my five year old daughter has a play set of brushes because she loves to play makeup but upon receiving them mommy is going to have to buy a second set for herself.

I received this product at a discounted price for my honest unbiased opinion.. [5.0/5] 7 Piece Professional makeup brush set 7 Piece Professional makeup brush set, I bought this to carry in my purse and wanted purple so its easy to find. It is made of great soft quality and a ribbon to secure brushes innside so that no dust or other environmental things get into the brushes. They are so soft and makeup or other beauty applied is easy as 1-2-3. I clean them with a tiny bowl in dawn dish soap or even with wessonoil. Yes cooking oil. If you like my simple review hit like. Jackie Paulson © 2015 [4.0/5] Great travel set Very cute brush set! Would be great for a travel set or something to put in your purse for on the go because of its variety and small size. I love the purple "ombré" effect on the brushes and the case is a great match.

The brushes themselves are relatively sturdy, nothing felt loose and I wasn't able to pull them apart. The bristles were soft, and they did a decent job of picking up and delivering powder which is one thing I really look for.

I really liked the case a lot too, not only is it very cute but it also holds all the brushes very well. The only issue you may run into would be a heavy plastic scent which is common with this type of case (it may smell like wet paint or just like chemicals). To fix this all you need to do is open up the case all the way and let it air out for a day or two (this is easy to do when you have the brushes out to wash).

Note I did receive this item at a discounted rate in exchange for my fair and honest review. All opinions expressed are solely my own and are completely honest. [4.0/5] For the price it's such a pretty case. The brushes are meh and not a complete set. I have 2 brush kits from this same seller and I'm going to be comparing them while I review them. I'll also be comparing to some other brushes I own. (I’m so sorry I don't have pictures for now, I’m having issues uploading them).

The other brush kit is:

[[ASIN:B011NX9QXE Aisxle 5 Piece Eye Makeup Brush Kit - Beauty and Travel Set - All In One - Soft Bristles and Easy to Carry - Easy to Use - Professional Eyeshadow Brush Makeup Kit - Full Set of Eye Brushes Whenever You Need Them - Perfect for Smokey Eye and More - Satisfaction Guaranteed]]

And to compare I have:

[[ASIN:B0030HMQTS EcoTools 6 Piece Essential Eye Brush Set]]
[[ASIN:B00GNA9K8O EcoTools Mini Essentials Brush Set]]
[[ASIN:B002CL84FI e.l.f. Cosmetics Eye Shadow Brush]]

The 5 piece brush kit (black) comes with the following:

Crease brush
Large flat eyeshadow brush
Small domed eyeshadow brush
Eyeliner brush (or for filling in eyebrows)
Pencil brush / smudger brush (could work as a lip brush too)
Metal tube case

The 7 piece brush kit (purple) comes with the following:
Blush brush
Large flat eyeshadow or concealer brush. Not sure
Small flat eyeshadow brush
Smudger or lip brush
Eyeliner brush
Sponge tip applicator
Eyebrow comb duo
Faux leather soft case

My opinion is that despite having less pieces, the brush set with the tube is the better one. The reason being that each piece has an intended distinct function yet they all work together to create a full eye look. Even compared to my trusty Ecotools I must admit this set is more complete (Ecotools eye set doesn't have an eyeliner brush). The other 7 piece kit has a mix of eye and face brushes but it’s not complete for either. Also, the black brushes from the smaller kit are softer and denser, and overall different than they purple counterparts. I would recommend the small kit as a complete basic eye set. The 7 piece set I would only recommend for the pretty case it comes in. Here’s some individual comparisons (sorry no pictures for now, I will update if I can fix the issue).

Both kits have a larger flat eyeshadow brush, which is good for applying color to the eyelid. The black one from the smaller kit is a little less wide, fluffier and denser. The purple one is very stiff and feels really similar to a small concealer brush instead of an eyeshadow brush. Both these brushes are smaller than the large eyeshadow brush from my Ecotools Eye kit and I appreciate that since the Ecotools covers almost all of my eyelid. They are both larger than the ELF $1 eyeshadow brush. I like that the black one is different from what I already own and will keep using it but the purple is just not good for eyes.

Both kits have a smaller eyeshadow brush. The purple one is flat and stiff just like the larger one, but the black one is really nice. It’s short and rounded top but dense and fluffy, like a mini version of the ELF brush. I like it for adding color to the inner part of the eyelid or creating detailed shapes. It’s also good for the lower lash line. Comparing it to my Ecotools kit it’s closest to the petite eye shading brush but it’s bigger and denser. I like both they are just different. This black brush will give more concentrated color than the less dense Ecotools.

Both kits have a pencil, smudger or lip type brush. The black one is rounder and better at being and eye brush, but the purple is again flatter and stiffer and better at being a lip brush. Comparing the black one to the smudger from Ecotools this one is smaller. I like both.

Both kits have an eyeliner brush. This brush I didn’t like in either set. It’s not as precise as Ecotools (from the mini essentials set) and it has less bristles so it’s a little scratchy.

As for difference the tube kit has a crease brush and the larger set doesn’t. It has a sponge tip, a blush brush and a brow comb instead. I feel like that crease brush is essential for a complete eye look. It’s big and fluffy and good for blending. It’s a little smaller and less bristles but comparable to the Ecotools angled crease brush. On the 7 piece kit you get nothing to blend your eyeshadow, all the eye brushes are stiff. The sponge tip is like a throwaway since you get those for free with any eyeshadow. I would be so happy to see it replaced with the angled brush. The blush brush is just ok. It sheds a little. I’m not a huge blush wearer so I don’t have another blush brush to compare but it’s similar in shape and size to the mini powder brush from the Ecotools mini set.

The case is the only thing that I really like about the 7 piece set. It’s very pretty. I feel like when you get this it’s because of the prettiness factor of the case and not so much the brush quality. Purple is my favorite color. I’m just going to take out the brushes I don’t like and replace them with the ones I do use that fit in it. The black tube case is ok and convenient, just not as pretty looking.

I hope this review was helpful.

Disclaimer: I purchased these 2 kits at a discount in exchange for an honest review. [5.0/5] Love these brushes These are the prettiest brushes around, love the softness. I will buy these for gifts, who wouldn't love to receive these as a present. Love them. [5.0/5] Love these makeup brushes Love these makeup brushes, so smooth and easy to apply my makeup! I love that you can choose from several different colors as well! [5.0/5] Great brushes! These makeup brushes have quickly become my favorite and go-to when applying makeup. They are soft on my skin, but firm enough to apply and contour perfectly. They are sturdy and I live the color! The case is also great to have for travel or just to carry in my purse!

response:
Most reviewers like brushes. Soft, cute, purple, good travel size. Case hold brushes well, some smell chemical, go away after air out. Good gift, good price, good beginner set. Wash before use.

Bad: one say cheap. Brushes not best quality, some shed, some stiff. Sponge tip useless. Case plastic-y.

One caveman do big compare: 5-piece black set better for eyes, more complete, softer, denser. 7-piece purple set pretty case but brushes stiffer, mix of face and eye brushes, not complete for either. Pretty case not make up for so-so brushes.
